title: Structured JSONL API Request Logging Middleware Pattern
summary: A minimal, zero-dependency structured logging pattern for API services. Each API endpoint call writes a JSONL record with timestamp, method, endpoint, status, params, and result summary (not full response body). The logger creates its directory on first write, tolerates OS errors (disk full, permission denied), and uses compact JSON separators to minimize log size. The pattern avoids logging secrets by design — it only logs params (query strings) and result IDs/counts, never full chunk content or request bodies.
why_reusable: Every API service needs request logging. This pattern is ~15 lines of Python with no dependencies, uses the standard library only, handles errors gracefully, and produces machine-parseable JSONL that can be analyzed with jq, grep, or any log tool.
adapter_target: Any FastAPI or Python web service — copy the log_api_request function and call it at each endpoint's return point.
implementation_notes: JSONL format: one JSON object per line, compact separators (no spaces, no newlines in values)
Automatic directory creation on first write (mkdir parents=True, exist_ok=True)
OSError-tolerant: if disk is full or permission denied, the API continues serving (log is best-effort)
Params and result are summarized — chunk_ids, counts, query strings — not full content
Timestamp in ISO 8601 UTC for cross-timezone consistency
Can be extended with request duration, client IP, user agent by adding fields to the event dict
tests_required: Unit test: log entry has all required fields
Unit test: log directory is created on first write
Unit test: OSError does not propagate (log is best-effort)
Unit test: JSONL output is valid JSON-per-line
Integration test: multiple concurrent writes do not corrupt the log file
risks: JSONL append is not atomic — concurrent writes from multiple workers may interleave lines
No log rotation — file grows indefinitely; need external rotation or periodic cleanup
No sensitive data filtering beyond what the caller chooses not to pass
OSError pass means silent log loss — monitor for missing log entries in production
do_not_do: Do not log full request bodies — they may contain secrets, PII, or large binary data
Do not log Authorization headers, cookies, or API keys
Do not let log failure block API response — log is always best-effort
Do not use without log rotation in production
notes: Extracted from DataBase backend_api/app/main.py log_api_request(). ~15 lines, zero dependencies, directly copyable.
